bsbe:which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models

reports.text:
Community 0 in IIT Jammu Biosciences & Bioengineering Department:
  Faculty: Santhilal Subhash
  PhDStudent: Isha Mukherjee, Shalini Gupta, Uma Mahesh
  ResearchArea: Goal, Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health, Key Focus Areas, Reproductive Biology, Genomics & Single Cell Biology, Multi-omics, Bioinformatics, Genomic and epigenomic-based tissue regulation in development and disease, Multi-omics and big data intergration to investigate tissue heterogeneity and cell plasticity, Developing AI/ML models utilizing NGS-based genomic data
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 13, SUPERVISED_BY: 3, STUDIES: 3

Summary: This group includes 1 faculty member(s): bsbe:Santhilal Subhash. 3 PhD student(s). working in areas like bsbe:Goal, bsbe:Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, bsbe:particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME).

members: bsbe:The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets
bsbe:the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics
bsbe:the lab employs a range of research techniques
bsbe:and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods
bsbe:we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways
bsbe:The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly

reports.text:
Community 7 in IIT Jammu Biosciences & Bioengineering Department:
  Faculty: Mithu Baidya
  FundingAgency: Startup Research Grant, DST-SERB, India Alliance DBT-Wellcome Trust, ICMR-IRG Grant
  PhDStudent: Abdul Rouf War, Madhurjya Protim Borah, Neeraj, Sarfraz Ahmed
  Project: bsbe:project:Research Project by Dr. Vinay Sharma (~ 32 Lacs) from Startu, bsbe:project:Research Project by Dr. Mithu Baidya (~ 134 Lacs) from India, bsbe:project:Research Project by Dr. Mithu Baidya (~ 28 Lacs) from Startu, bsbe:project:Research Project by Dr. Mithu Baidya (~ 141 Lacs) from ICMR-
  ResearchArea: The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics, To achieve this, the lab employs a range of research techniques, including biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods, we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways, The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly, Molecular Pharmacology, Drug Discovery, Structure based drug designing, Receptor cell biology
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 14, PRINCIPAL_INVESTIGATOR: 3, FUNDED_BY: 4, PI_IS: 3, SUPERVISED_BY: 4, STUDIES: 4

GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, bsbe:the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics, bsbe:To achieve this.
